nodes.label: AI as decision-making support
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary when expertise suffices
nodes.grounding_span: AI can be helpful to make a reasonable first pass guess to establish a starting point for screening, reducing mental load and choice paralysis

nodes.label: AI for data analysis and report writing
nodes.label_negative: Human manual report writing prone to errors
nodes.grounding_span: it would be nice if AI would help me write the report. It is very robotic and regulated, and seems like a prime candidate to be handled by an unfeeling machine

nodes.label: Institutional permission for AI use
nodes.label_negative: Institutional prohibition or uncertainty about AI
nodes.grounding_span: I would use it more if it was encouraged or explicitly allowed. But as is, I can't really tell my boss that I used AI for a report

nodes.label: AI as fallback when own solutions fail
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary when own solutions work
nodes.grounding_span: I likely would have turned to AI if my own solutions had failed, but because everything I did seemed to work

nodes.label: Efficiency and reduced cognitive load
nodes.grounding_span: reducing mental load and choice paralysis

nodes.label: Regulatory compliance and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: it is naturally difficult for human beings to follow rules down to the letter without making any small mistakes

nodes.label: Risk avoidance
nodes.grounding_span: I can't really tell my boss that I used AI for a report, so I just won't do it to avoid getting in trouble

nodes.label: Positive toward AI for initial screening
nodes.grounding_span: AI can be helpful to make a reasonable first pass guess

nodes.label: Positive toward AI for report writing
nodes.grounding_span: it would be nice if AI would help me write the report

nodes.label: Cautious avoidance due to institutional uncertainty
nodes.grounding_span: I can't really tell my boss that I used AI for a report, so I just won't do it to avoid getting in trouble

nodes.label: Confidence in AI as a key skill
nodes.grounding_span: Everything else I felt like I knew what I was doing and I didn't need to ask

nodes.label: Selective, need-based engagement with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I was more confident in the other steps, and I was overwhelmed by the number of choices for column

nodes.label: Pragmatic risk-averse tool adoption
nodes.grounding_span: I only feel comfortable using it like Google and without including any confidential information
